topic.what: Systemic vulnerabilities where both Core LLM and Reward Model fail simultaneously in RLHF pipelines
topic.how: Safety Mentor generates compositional adversarial prompts; hierarchical adaptive sampling discovers weaknesses; two-stage repair fixes RM then Core LLM
topic.why_matters: Existing red-teaming targets only policy-level weaknesses and treats RM as perfect evaluator, missing coupled failures where both components fail together leaving no internal safety mechanism

characteristics.context: After one-shot ARES repair, vulnerability discovery rate plummeted from 63.5% to 4.3%

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of safe responses on adversarial benchmarks
characteristics.context: ARES achieves 0.97 StrongReject 0.95 HarmBench 0.96 RedTeam vs Initial RLHF 0.79/0.75/0.74

characteristics.what_itasures: Incorrect refusal rate on benign queries
characteristics.context: Over-refusal rate maintained at 0.10 vs 0.07 baseline showing safety-utility balance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational time for adversarial data generation
characteristics.context: ARES generates 4000 samples in 6.75 hours vs APRT 28 hours, 4x faster

characteristics.what_it_measures: Training samples needed to reach safety targets
characteristics.context: ARES matches full PKU-SafeRLHF safety (0.94 StrongReject) with 4k vs 10.8k samples

requires: Safety Mentor LLM for prompt generation

novelty: First framework targeting coupled Core LLM + RM systemic failures
Compositional attack generation via topic-persona-goal-tactic taxonomy
Hierarchical adaptive sampling with two-level reinforcement
Two-stage sequential repair (RM then Core LLM) for holistic alignment
open_problems: Coverage limited to single-turn text interactions
Generalization to tool use code generation multi-agent scenarios
Trade-off between zero-vulnerability and utility degradation
